summary: eth-wallet-sentinel exfiltrates host and environment data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: Package contains a postinstall/preinstall lifecycle command in package.json (line 30) that uses child_process and Node's https module to POST hostname, environment variables, and host identity to https://ddjidd564.github.io. A sibling scanner.js file extends this behavior: it imports fs, os, https, and child_process, walks the filesystem (fs.existsSync, process.cwd), collects os.hostname/version/user (whoami) and other system intel, and sends it via https.request POST to the same hardcoded https://ddjidd564.github.io endpoint (lines 142-161, 234-249, 332-337, 371). The destination is a personal GitHub Pages site unrelated to the package's stated 'wallet sentinel' purpose, with no relation to a documented publisher. Trigger fires both at install time (package.json lifecycle) and on script execution. This is a credential/system-information exfiltration beacon to attacker-controlled infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
